flowcharts.title: Clinical Stage I PM with Epithelioid Histology - Surgical Evaluation and Treatment

nodes.label: Clinical stage I PM with epithelioid histology, PS 0-2, medically operable. Adjuvant: If surgery is pursued, systemic therapy can be given as either induction or adjuvant therapy. Sequential pleural IMRT at a center with experience may be considered after surgery and/or systemic therapy.
Clinical stage I PM with epithelioid histology, PS 0-2, asymptomatic with minimal disease burden
Systemic therapy (preferred primary treatment): Systemic therapy is the preferred primary treatment for clinical stage I PM with epithelioid histology. Pleural IMRT may be considered sequentially.

Surgical evaluation for potential resection: Surgical evaluation should be considered only for select patients. The benefit of surgical resection is unclear. If surgery is deemed viable, P/D is recommended over EPP. FDG-PET/CT and invasive mediastinal staging required before surgery.
Observation for progression: Observation may be considered if systemic therapy is planned at the time of symptomatic or radiographic progression.

flowcharts.title: Clinical Stage I PM with Epithelioid Histology - Surgical Treatment Pathway

nodes.label: Surgical exploration reveals resectable disease. Adjuvant: After recovery from surgery, patients should be referred for adjuvant therapy, which may include chemotherapy and RT.
Pleurectomy/decortication (P/D): P/D is the preferred surgical treatment option. Consider sequential pleural IMRT after P/D.
Sequential pleural IMRT: Consider sequential pleural IMRT at centers with experience. Should only be performed in carefully selected patients at centers with experience.
Systemic therapy and consider sequential pleural IMRT: Systemic therapy and consider sequential pleural IMRT.

flowcharts.title: Clinical Stage II-IV PM with Epithelioid Histology; Sarcomatoid or Biphasic Histology (Any Stage); or Medically Inoperable

nodes.label: Clinical stage II-IV with epithelioid histology OR sarcomatoid/biphasic histology at any stage OR medically inoperable any stage; PS 0-2
Asymptomatic with minimal disease burden, PS 0-2
First-line systemic therapy: Systemic therapy is the primary treatment. ICI-based regimens are favored for non-epithelioid histology.

Observation for progression: Observation may be considered for patients who are not eligible for surgery and are asymptomatic with minimal burden of disease if systemic therapy is planned when progression occurs.
